name: Recipe Crafting
description: A player converts specific ingredients into defined outputs by executing a validated recipe transaction.
design_notes: Recipe crafting is a transaction boundary between recipe data, item instances, inventory capacity, unlocks, and output grants. The useful design work is deciding which recipe_id is available, which ingredient_item_id entries are consumed, what happens when storage is full, and how failed or canceled transactions avoid duplicating outputs.
design_purpose: Turn gathered materials into useful tools, consumables, structures, or progression items.
Give players planning goals by making ingredients, stations, and unlocks visible before they craft.
Provide a deterministic conversion rule that economies, quests, and survival loops can depend on.
player_fantasy: The player feels resourceful when collected parts become a chosen item through a reliable process.

parameters.name: recipe_id
parameters.description: Stable recipe identifier used for lookup, migration, analytics, and UI preview.

parameters.name: ingredient_item_ids
parameters.description: Item identifiers and counts required before the transaction can consume inventory entries.

parameters.name: output_item_id
parameters.description: Item identifier granted when validation, consumption, and capacity checks all succeed.

parameters.name: output_quantity
parameters.description: Stack count or number of item instances produced by a single successful recipe transaction.

parameters.name: required_unlock_id
parameters.description: Progression gate that must be satisfied before the recipe appears or can be confirmed.

parameters.name: capacity_policy_id
parameters.description: Rule for handling successful recipes when the target inventory lacks room for the output.

edge_cases: The player confirms a recipe while another transaction removes one required ingredient from the inventory.
The recipe is migrated between content versions and old save data references an obsolete recipe_id.
The output stack cannot fit after ingredients have already been reserved for consumption.
A multiplayer client predicts the craft locally but the server rejects the unlock or station requirement.
common_bugs: Ingredients are consumed but the output is not granted because capacity validation runs after deletion.
Rapid double-clicks submit two transaction_id values and produce duplicate outputs from one ingredient set.
Recipe previews use display-only substitution rules that do not match server validation.
Crafted item instances miss durability, quality tier, ownership, or binding metadata during creation.
balancing_notes: Recipe costs should create meaningful gathering goals without forcing players into repetitive low-value collection.
Recipes that unlock core survival tools should be discoverable early and not depend on rare random drops.
Batch crafting can reduce friction, but large batches may bypass storage pressure or economy sinks.
accessibility_notes: Show missing ingredients, station requirements, and inventory capacity failures in text as well as icons.
Allow confirmation flows that do not require repeated rapid clicks for large batches.

implementation_notes.unity: Keep recipe_id data in ScriptableObjects or Addressable data, but execute crafting through a transaction service that reserves ingredient item_instance_id entries, checks capacity_policy_id, grants output_item_id, then commits or rolls back as one operation.
implementation_notes.godot: Store recipe definitions as Resources and run confirmations through an inventory service instead of UI nodes. The transaction should validate required_unlock_id, consume ingredient_item_ids, create output instances, and emit one result event for Control UI refresh.
implementation_notes.unreal: Data Tables or Primary Data Assets can hold recipe definitions, while an inventory component owns the server-authoritative transaction. Validate recipe_id, station access, unlock tags, capacity, and output metadata on the server before replicating item grants.
implementation_notes.web: Represent recipes as immutable data and inventory entries as item_instance_id records. In Phaser or custom JavaScript, keep UI preview read-only; the confirm handler should submit one transaction_id and reject duplicate pending submissions.

relationships.reason: Recipe crafting needs item instance slots, stack counts, and capacity checks before ingredients are consumed.
Recipes usually spend crafting materials as validated ingredient inputs.
Crafting unlocks control which recipes appear and can be committed by the player.

example_games.title: Minecraft
example_games.note: Uses recipe-based material conversion for tools, blocks, and survival items.

example_games.title: The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt
example_games.note: Uses recipes to craft alchemy items, gear, and consumables from gathered materials.

example_games.title: Subnautica
example_games.note: Uses recipes to turn gathered materials into equipment and base components.

example_games.title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim
example_games.note: Uses ingredient and material recipes for alchemy, smithing, and cooking.

example_games.title: Alien: Isolation
example_games.note: Uses crafted tools and consumables from gathered materials during survival play.

example_games.title: Terraria
example_games.note: Uses recipe crafting to convert gathered materials into equipment, tools, and blocks.

example_games.title: Stardew Valley
example_games.note: Uses recipes to craft equipment, machines, consumables, and farm items from gathered materials.
